rationale: Gold snapped four consecutive losing weeks after a soft June NFP pulled forward September Fed-cut expectations; the DXY breaking below 102 (a multi-year low) and 10y real yields turning negative (-0.45%) form a coherent pro-gold macro triad that is fresh rather than priced-in, while the prior selloff from 52w highs absorbed the Goldman downgrade and hawkish-Fed narrative. The main near-term risk is this week's FOMC minutes reasserting a no-cut stance, which caps conviction and warrants sizing discipline.
key_drivers: 10y real yields negative (-0.45%), classic gold tailwind
Soft June NFP repricing September Fed cut
First weekly gain after 4 losses — short-squeeze/mean-reversion setup
Geopolitical safe-haven bid (Middle East + Eastern Europe)
FOMC minutes risk (hawkish re-assertion could reverse bounce)
